analysis.device: NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition

operators: aten::mm gate-projection cohort (~255 operator records op_id 955..1465, each 2 kernels: cutlass_80_simt_sgemm_128x32_8x5_tn_align1 + cublasLt::splitKreduce_kernel)
layer::unique::prologue (is_fused node containing the first forward pass: hundreds of unrolled-timestep SGEMMs + fused gate pointwise kernels + mean reduction)

bottleneck.description: torch.compile/Inductor unrolled the 2-layer LSTM into per-timestep primitives. The dominant kernel is cutlass_80_simt_sgemm_128x32_8x5_tn_align1: the _simt_sgemm_ token means cuBLAS/cutlass selected the scalar FP32 SIMT multiply-accumulate pipeline, NOT Tensor Cores. Every gate-GEMM operator reports smsp__pipe_tensor_cycles_active.avg.pct_of_peak_sustained_active = 0.0 and aggregated.tensor_core_active_pct = 0.0, so every Blackwell Tensor Core is idle across ~94% of attributed trace time. The problem shape is [32,512]x[512,2048]: K=512 and N=2048 (4 gates x 512 hidden) are healthy but M=32 (batch) is tiny, forcing the 128x32 SIMT tile to a grid of [8,2,8]=128 CTAs (<1 wave on ~188 SMs), achieved_occupancy 7.9-8.5%, sm_throughput ~16%, eligible_cycles_pct ~26% (latency/launch-bound proxy on Blackwell where warp_cycles_per_instruction is removed). dram_throughput is only ~25% with l2_hit_rate ~71% so the op is NOT memory-bound: it is a scalar-FP32, under-occupied, launch-overhead-dominated GEMM. The strongest evidence for the remedy is in warnings[]: the eager/reference run of this exact model (k_00425..k_00532) used void elemWiseRNNcell<...cudnnRNNMode...> plus cutlass_80_tensorop_s1688gemm_64x64_32x6_tn_align4 -- a cuDNN FUSED RNN path with a Tensor Core (s1688 = tf32/bf16 tensorop) GEMM that batches all four gates and persists state across timesteps. Inductor's decomposition discarded that fused path and replaced a handful of fused Tensor Core kernels with ~1020 tiny SIMT SGEMM + splitKreduce launches.

transformation.description: Keep the recurrent module on the cuDNN fused RNN op instead of letting Inductor unroll it into a per-timestep aten.mm loop. Preferred route: author the model with nn.LSTM (it already is) and run the LSTM submodule eagerly while compiling only the mean-pool + classifier head, so cuDNN's fused elemWiseRNNcell + cutlass_80_tensorop_s1688gemm kernels are used (the profiler shows they are available on this device). At the functional IR level this substitutes the entire unrolled aten.mm subgraph with a single aten.lstm / cudnn_rnn op: one persistent fused kernel sequence engaging Tensor Cores, replacing ~1020 SIMT SGEMM+splitKreduce launches. NOTE on match_target: the schema enum has no LSTM entry; F.scaled_dot_product_attention is the closest sequence-op enum value, but the true transform site is the nn.LSTM / aten.lstm call before Dynamo decomposes it.
transformation.location: Functional level: the nn.LSTM / aten::lstm call site, before Dynamo/AOTAutograd decomposes it into the per-timestep gate-matmul loop. Achieved by excluding the recurrent module from the compiled region (compile the head only), or by registering the LSTM as a non-decomposed op.
fx_steps: # Route A (preferred): compile only the non-recurrent head; run nn.LSTM eagerly
# so cuDNN's fused elemWiseRNNcell + tensorop s1688 GEMM kernels run.
self.lstm = lstm                       # eager cuDNN fused RNN
out, _ = self.lstm(x)                  # one fused cudnn_rnn call
# Route B (if the recurrent region MUST stay inside the compiled graph):
# prevent Dynamo from tracing into / unrolling the RNN so it lowers to aten.lstm.
dynamo.allow_in_graph(torch.lstm)  # keep as a single graph op, not unrolled
# then compile the whole model; verify nsys shows elemWiseRNNcell instead of
transformation.code_hint:
# Verify success in nsys: the LSTM region should show a small number of
# elemWiseRNNcell<...cudnnRNNMode...> + cutlass_80_tensorop_s1688gemm_64x64_*
# kernels (Tensor Core) replacing the ~510 cutlass_80_simt_sgemm_128x32 +
# ~510 cublasLt::splitKreduce_kernel pairs. torch.backends.cudnn.enabled must
# be True (it is on this capture). This is the single largest structural win.

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: cuDNN fused RNN keeps the four gate activations and cell state in registers/SMEM across the elementwise update, eliminating the per-timestep round-trip writes of gate slices to HBM that the unrolled aten path performs, and removes ~510 splitKreduce epilogue launches entirely. Also engages the bf16/tf32 Tensor Core MMA pipeline that is 0% utilized today.

optimizations.notes: Largest win but changes the execution backend for the recurrent region rather than transforming kernels in place, so it is MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE with OPT-2/OPT-3 over the same recurrent matmuls -- adopt this OR the in-place GEMM transforms, not both on the same nodes. Confidence medium because exact cuDNN kernel selection and Blackwell s1688 tensorop availability depend on the runtime cuDNN version; warnings[] confirms the fused Tensor Core path was actually selected on this device. Estimate is a relative within-profile comparison; duration_ns is 2-5x ncu-inflated.

operators: aten::mm gate-projection cohort: the input-to-hidden half (~127 operator records per layer) computing W_ih @ x_t, sizes [[32,512],[512,2048],[32,2048]]

bottleneck.description: If the unrolled cell loop is kept (i.e. OPT-1 is not adopted), the recurrence issues two [32,512]x[512,2048] SGEMMs per timestep per layer: the input-to-hidden projection W_ih @ x_t and the hidden-to-hidden projection W_hh @ h_{t-1}. The operator input_op_ids chain (...953->955->957->959...) shows each timestep's gate matmul consuming the previous timestep's output -- the graph is fully serialized into ~128 dependent launches per layer. But only the hidden-to-hidden term is genuinely recurrent; W_ih @ x_t depends only on the input sequence and is independent across timesteps. Computing it one timestep at a time yields a degenerate M=32 GEMM at ~8% occupancy / ~16% SM throughput on a 128-CTA grid, each followed by a splitKreduce_kernel that is pure launch overhead at this size. Batching all 128 timesteps of the input projection into one [128*32,512]x[512,2048]=[4096,512]x[512,2048] GEMM raises M from 32 to 4096, turning ~127 tiny under-occupied launches per layer into one large well-tiled GEMM that saturates the SMs and eliminates ~127 splitKreduce launches per layer.

transformation.description: Hoist the input-to-hidden gate projection out of the recurrence. At the functional (pre-AOTAutograd) IR level, rewrite each LSTM layer to (1) compute Wx = F.linear(x_seq.reshape(seq*batch, in), W_ih, b_ih) once as a single [seq*batch, 4*hidden] GEMM and reshape back to [seq, batch, 4*hidden], then (2) run the recurrence performing only the hidden-to-hidden F.linear(h_{t-1}, W_hh) and adding the precomputed Wx[t]. This is the standard 'precompute input projections' LSTM transform; the hidden-to-hidden term stays sequential because of the genuine dependence on h_{t-1}.
transformation.location: Dynamo FX graph (pre-AOTAutograd decomposition), or preferably the nn.Module source: the per-timestep input-projection F.linear calls share one W_ih weight node and slice the same input sequence.
fx_steps: #     def forward(self, x_seq, state):          # x_seq: [seq, batch, in]
#         Wx = Wx.view(seq, b, 4 * self.hidden)  # ONE [seq*b,in]x[in,4h] GEMM
#             i, f, g, o = gates.chunk(4, dim=-1)
if node.op == 'call_function' and node.target is torch.nn.functional.linear:
# A weight reused across many timesteps whose activations are independent
# slices of one input tensor is the hoistable input projection; concatenate
# those activations, run one F.linear, index the result per timestep.
transformation.code_hint:
# Only the input-to-hidden projection is hoistable; the hidden-to-hidden term
# is data-dependent and stays a per-timestep [32,512]x[512,2048] GEMM (still
# SIMT-bound, addressed by OPT-3). Numerically equivalent to the original LSTM.

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: Reads each W_ih weight matrix once per layer instead of once per timestep, and eliminates ~127 splitKreduce launches per layer. Activation traffic is unchanged but coalesced into one large GEMM with far better SM utilization.

optimizations.notes: Must be applied at functional level before AOTAutograd decomposition (after decomposition the shared-weight identity across timesteps is obscured by per-timestep aten.view/reshape nodes). Combined with OPT-3 the hoisted [4096,512]x[512,2048] input GEMM is large enough to fully utilize Blackwell Tensor Cores. Mutually exclusive with OPT-1 over the recurrent matmuls (OPT-1 replaces the whole loop with cuDNN); apply OPT-2+OPT-3 only if the unrolled custom-cell loop is retained.

operators: aten::mm gate-projection cohort (~255 records, sizes [[32,512],[512,2048],[32,2048]]) across 2 LSTM layers
layer::unique::prologue (the gate SGEMMs inside the first forward pass)

bottleneck.description: If the unrolled loop is retained, every remaining gate projection still dispatches to the FP32 SIMT scalar path (cutlass_80_simt_sgemm), with smsp__pipe_tensor_cycles_active = 0.0 and tensor_core_active_pct = 0.0 on every record. Because the operands are float32, cuBLAS/cutlass cannot engage the Blackwell tensor pipeline at all. Promoting the GEMM operands to bf16 (or enabling TF32) routes selection to a cutlass tensorop kernel (the cutlass_80_tensorop_s1688gemm path the reference run in warnings already used), engaging Tensor Cores. The M=32 tile inefficiency caps the per-GEMM Tensor Core utilization, so the hidden-to-hidden term alone gains ~2-4x; combined with OPT-2 (which raises M to seq*batch for the input projection) the hoisted GEMM gains substantially more.

transformation.description: Promote the gate-projection GEMMs to bfloat16 so cuBLAS/cutlass selects a tensorop GEMM. The aten-level FX pass inserts aten._to_copy casts to bfloat16 on the matmul operands of every aten.mm.default / aten.addmm.default whose operands are float32, then casts the result back to float32 to preserve the recurrent-state dtype contract that downstream sigmoid/tanh/mul cell ops depend on. Inductor CSE folds the repeated constant W_ih/W_hh weight casts, so per-timestep overhead is one activation cast, dwarfed by the Tensor Core GEMM speedup. Lowest-risk variant: enable TF32 (torch.backends.cuda.matmul.allow_tf32) which keeps FP32 accumulation and still routes to cutlass_80_tensorop_s1688gemm with no FX surgery.
transformation.location: Aten-level FX graph (post-AOTAutograd decomposition): every aten.mm.default and aten.addmm.default node whose matmul operands are float32.
fx_steps: if node.op != 'call_function' or node.target not in TARGETS:
mat_idx = (0, 1) if node.target is torch.ops.aten.mm.default else (1, 2)
transformation.code_hint:
# Lowest-risk first step (no FX surgery, FP32 accumulation preserved):
#   torch.backends.cuda.matmul.allow_tf32 = True
#   torch.backends.cudnn.allow_tf32 = True   # before torch.compile
# This alone routes the SGEMM to cutlass_80_tensorop_s1688gemm (TF32).
# Full bf16 inference fast path: model.eval().bfloat16(); x = x.bfloat16().

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: Halves operand byte traffic (FP32->BF16) and replaces the SIMT FP32 arithmetic pipeline with the bf16/tf32 Tensor Core MMA pipeline. Primary gain is compute throughput, not DRAM (these GEMMs are only ~25% DRAM, 71% L2-resident). The lower-register tensorop kernel also lifts achieved_occupancy above the current ~8%.

optimizations.notes: Assumes inference-only (no autograd). Mutually exclusive with OPT-1 over the same nodes (OPT-1 replaces the loop with cuDNN, which already runs the s1688 tensorop GEMM). Apply with OPT-2 if the unrolled loop is retained. The M=32 tile inefficiency limits standalone gains on the hidden-to-hidden term; OPT-2 compounds it. allow_tf32 is the conservative variant preserving FP32 accumulation.

operators: All aten::mm / aten::addmm weight parameter nodes (W_ih, W_hh per layer; classifier weight) feeding the gate GEMMs, plus their cublasLt::splitKreduce_kernel epilogues

bottleneck.description: Two compile-time-decidable costs ride on every gate GEMM. First, each [32,512]x[512,2048] SGEMM is followed by a cublasLt::splitKreduce_kernel (grid [64,2,1]): cuBLAS split the K=512 dimension across CTAs and needs a reduction epilogue, which for M=32 is pure overhead -- ~510 such reduce launches across the trace, each ~1760 ns with no useful metrics. max_autotune lets Inductor benchmark a non-split-K tile (or a Triton GEMM) that produces the result in one kernel. Second, weights are not frozen, so the _tn_ transposed-normal layout cuBLAS wants (encoded in the kernel's _tn_ suffix) is materialized per call and weight guards are re-checked each forward. freezing constant-folds the weights and hoists the transpose. These are incremental, zero-risk-for-inference gains that stack on top of OPT-2/OPT-3.

transformation.description: Enable freezing and max_autotune so Inductor treats the LSTM weight parameters as compile-time constants (folding the _tn_ transpose and dropping per-call weight guards) and autotunes a GEMM configuration that avoids the split-K reduction epilogue for these small-M shapes. With OPT-3's bf16/tf32 weights in place, freezing additionally lets Inductor emit a pre-packed tensorop weight layout for maximum Tensor Core tile reuse.
transformation.location: torch.compile options dict / torch._inductor.config, applied during Inductor lowering.
transformation.code_hint:
# Requires model.eval(); freezing is incompatible with training.
# First call triggers autotune benchmarking (slow); result is cached and reused
# for every identical-shape timestep GEMM, so the one-time cost amortizes.
# torch._inductor.config.autotune_local_cache = True  # persist across runs.

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: Eliminates the per-GEMM split-K reduction epilogue kernels and the runtime weight transpose/guard work. After OPT-3, enables the pre-packed bf16/tf32 tensorop weight layout.

optimizations.notes: By backend funnel ordering (functional -> aten -> inductor_config), OPT-2 and OPT-3 are already applied when freezing runs, so the pre-packed tensorop layout is emitted with no explicit within-level prerequisite. The splitKreduce elimination depends on the autotuner finding a non-split-K tile competitive at M=32; impact is workload-specific. Incompatible with torch.compile(dynamic=True) if seq_len or batch vary. If OPT-1 (cuDNN fused LSTM) is adopted, this pass applies only to the classifier head.

global_notes: Time budget derived from operator aggregated.total_duration_ns. The profile contains: (a) one is_fused operator layer::unique::prologue holding the entire FIRST forward pass (setup transpose/clone/zeros/unbind, hundreds of unrolled-timestep cutlass_80_simt_sgemm + splitKreduce + triton_poi_fused gate-pointwise kernels, and the mean reduction), and (b) ~255 granular per-timestep aten::mm operator records (op_id 955,957,959,... each [[32,512],[512,2048],[32,2048]], 2 kernels: SGEMM + splitKreduce, aggregated total_duration_ns clustered tightly at 8416-8448 ns). The gate-GEMM cohort dominates attributed time; the classifier addmm and mean pooling are <0.1%. Time-budget pct values are estimated from the dominant operator population; absolute ns are ncu-inflated and used only for relative comparison.

1 operator class below the 1% threshold omitted (combined ~0.05% of attributed time): aten::addmm classifier [[32,512],[512,10]] and the at::native::reduce mean-pool kernel. Retained as a single time_budget line for completeness, not reasoned about as a bottleneck.

hardware-limits.md not found on disk (searched /home/ubuntu/Profiler/knowledge and adjacent paths). Device NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition (GB202 / sm_100 class) properties inferred from published Blackwell architecture: ~188 SMs, 128 FP32 cores/SM. The gate-GEMM grid is [8,2,8]=128 CTAs, i.e. <1 wave, confirming severe wave underutilization at M=32 independent of the exact SM count; wave/occupancy conclusions are robust to a +/-20% SM-count error. A100 SXM5 fallback was NOT used because device_name is present.

warp_cycles_per_instruction is null for every operator (counter removed on Blackwell sm_100). Latency-bound proxy applied per spec: eligible_cycles_pct < ~20-30% indicates latency/launch-bound. Gate GEMMs report eligible_cycles_pct ~26%, achieved_occupancy ~8.2%, dram_throughput ~25% (NOT memory-bound), tensor_core_active_pct 0% -- the regime is small-M scalar-FP32 SIMT GEMM that cannot fill the machine, dominated by per-launch overhead and the SIMT arithmetic pipeline.

fused_kernel_double_count flag ACTIVE: layer::unique::prologue has is_fused=true and encapsulates the first forward pass. Its constituent gate-SGEMM/splitKreduce/triton kernels are counted once inside the prologue line item and are NOT double-counted against the granular aten::mm cohort (op_id >= 955), which represents the steady-state timesteps. Reported launch/duration totals for the cohort exclude the prologue's contents to avoid double counting.

Edge-case flags NOT raised: multi_stream_overlap (all kernels stream_id=7); cuda_graph_replay (compile_mode=inductor, not cudagraphs); dynamic_shapes (per-op aggregated total_duration_ns is tightly clustered ~8416-8448 ns, CV << 50%); high_unattributed (no unattributed_kernels array; 0 unattributed per the capture); clock_domain (start_ns ~8.1e9, positive, < 1e15). warm_up_inflation could not be evaluated because capture_metadata has no measure_iters field; kernel_count per granular operator is 2 (1 SGEMM + 1 splitKreduce), consistent with steady-state and not indicative of warm-up bleed.

The granular aten::mm cohort is the unrolled stacked LSTM: input_op_ids form a strict chain (...953->955->957->959...), each timestep consuming the prior hidden state. Sizes are uniformly [[32,512],[512,2048],[32,2048]] = (batch=32, hidden=512, 4 gates x 512 = 2048). Two GEMMs per cell (input-to-hidden + hidden-to-hidden) x ~128 timesteps x 2 layers, each paired with a cublasLt::splitKreduce_kernel.

Direct evidence for OPT-1 is in warnings[] (k_00425..k_00532, 'flagged as initialization kernel'): the eager/reference run used void elemWiseRNNcell<...cudnnRNNMode...> + cutlass_80_tensorop_s1688gemm_64x64_32x6_tn_align4 -- a cuDNN FUSED RNN path with a Tensor Core GEMM. These are warm-up/uncompiled kernels correctly excluded from attribution. Their presence proves a Tensor Core fused RNN path exists on this device that Inductor's decomposition discarded.

Recommended application: choose ONE of two mutually-exclusive routes for the recurrent region. Route 1 (largest, structural): OPT-1 -- run nn.LSTM eagerly to restore the cuDNN fused Tensor Core RNN path; then OPT-4 freezing applies to the classifier head. Route 2 (in-place, lower integration risk if the model must stay a compiled custom cell loop): OPT-2 (functional, hoist input projection, raises M to seq*batch) -> OPT-3 (aten, bf16/tf32 to engage Tensor Cores) -> OPT-4 (inductor_config, freezing + max_autotune to drop split-K epilogues). Cross-level ordering (functional -> aten -> inductor_config) is enforced by the backend funnel; no within-level prerequisites needed. Do NOT sum impact across the two routes -- they target the same nodes by different mechanisms.

duration_ns values are 2-5x inflated by ncu application-replay counter-collection overhead. All estimated_impact.relative_duration_reduction_ns and total_profiled_wall_time_ms are relative within-profile comparisons, not absolute wall-clock predictions.
